steps.title: Extract the frequency of igneous rocks
steps.content: From the frequency table, the number of igneous rocks is 10.

steps.title: Identify the total number of rocks
steps.content: The problem states there are 70 rocks in the collection.

steps.title: Calculate the probability and identify the choice
steps.content:
Apply the probability formula with igneous rocks as favorable outcomes and total rocks as the sample space:
$$P(\text{igneous}) = \frac{10}{70}$$
That confirms D is correct.
